title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Language Lens: Feed Go Schema Constraints
summary: The database uses URLs as mutable primary keys, repeats publisher strings, permits cross-tenant references, lacks temporal constraints, and indexes without query evidence. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go schema constraints increment in constrained relational feed-service schema. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Schema Constraints · Theory Feed Go Schema Constraints
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7870 during an accessible community-news reader review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, compression, entity, depth, timeout, or publisher-budget failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.

checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
